prompt:
I play one-suit Spider on paper to unwind and I want a strict digital referee for it — class Spider in Spider.java, plain Java, stdlib only. Ranks are the tokens A 2 3 4 5 6 7 8 9 T J Q K (ace low); with one suit there are eight copies of each rank, 104 cards.

Two ways in. Spider.fromDeck(text) deals a fresh game from a deck fixture (see decks/club.deck, whitespace-separated ranks, top of the pack first): the first 54 cards go face-down round-robin — card 1 to column 1, card 2 to column 2, ... card 11 back on column 1 — so columns 1-4 end up six deep and 5-10 five deep, then each column's last-dealt card is turned face up. The remaining 50 wait as five stock rows of ten; each later deal() takes the next ten cards, one onto each column left to right, face up. Reject decks that aren't exactly 104 known tokens with exactly eight of each rank. Spider.fromLayout(text) restores a saved position (see layouts/): a 'removed N' line (0-8 cleared runs), zero or more 'deal ...' lines of exactly ten ranks (first line is the next deal), and exactly ten 'col <face-down...> | <face-up...>' lines, bottom of pile first. Validate hard: unknown tokens, missing '|', wrong column count, removed out of range, a non-empty pile with nothing face up — and bookkeeping: cards on the table plus stock plus 13 per removed run must come to 104 with each rank adding up to eight. All rejects are IllegalArgumentException.

Play. move(src, count, dst) lifts the top `count` face-up cards of column src as a block onto dst: the block must read as a strict descending run (each card exactly one lower than the one beneath it), and it may land only on an empty column or on a top card exactly one rank above the block's bottom card. Column numbers are 1-10, src != dst, count at least 1 and no deeper than the face-up cards; anything else is IllegalArgumentException and leaves the board alone. When a lift exposes a face-down card it flips face up. Whenever a column's top thirteen face-up cards run K down to A, the run is cleared off immediately (removed goes up one, and whatever it uncovers flips) — that check runs after every landing and after every dealt row, any column. deal() is IllegalStateException if the stock is out — or, house rule I care about most, if ANY column is empty: you may never deal across a gap. won() when all eight runs are gone; removed() counts them.

render() gives the position back, no trailing newline: a 'stock <rows> removed <n>' line then ten lines '<col>: <cards bottom first>' with face-down cards as '?', empty columns as '-'. Spider.replay(game, commands) runs a script of 'm src count dst' / 'd' commands and returns a transcript: '=== start ===' plus the render, then per command a '> <cmd>' echo plus the render after it, blocks separated by blank lines, ending 'result: won' or 'result: playing', every line newline-terminated; bad commands ('x ...', wrong arity) are IllegalArgumentException and engine refusals bubble out unchanged.

TestMain.java pins fixture games from decks/ and layouts/ — 'java TestMain.java' is the gate. Tests and fixtures are read-only.
notes: 7G6 item 2: one-suit Spider. Deck fixture is eight A..K sets back-to-back so every dealt pile position is derivable by hand; all pinned renders (fresh deal, flips, deal row, run-clear) were computed from deal arithmetic before the reference existed (reference passed 27/27 first run). Layout fixtures audited by script: card totals and per-rank counts reconcile to 8 decks minus removed runs. Pins: round-robin deal shape (6/6/6/6/5...), descending same-suit lift rule, landing rule, empty-column deal refusal (the catalog-pinned house rule, tested twice incl. after partially filling gaps), stock-empty refusal, run removal on move AND on deal with flip-after-clear, 12-card near-run not removed, win detection, replay transcript grammar, load-time card/rank reconciliation. Domain check: py-klondike and rust-freecell are the existing solitaires — different games (different legality/lift/deal rules); differentiation noted in catalog rotation law.
